summary: Activate passive MCPs as live skills: supermemory, sequentialthinking, websearch, and grep are now registered in registry.json with triggers, synergies, and categories; enabled in compound-engineering.json; and have dedicated SKILL.md files with explicit invocation patterns. skill-orchestrator-runtime SKILL.md extended with detection rules for all four domains. Removed dead tavily skill entry and stale github/tavily references from profiles and categories.

changes.description: 136-line skill definition for supermemory MCP covering persistent memory recall, storage, and cross-session context
94-line skill definition for sequentialthinking MCP covering structured step-by-step reasoning and hypothesis testing
72-line skill definition for websearch MCP covering live web research, page extraction, and content analysis
82-line skill definition for grep MCP covering external code search across public repositories for real-world examples

Registered supermemory, sequentialthinking, websearch, grep with category/triggers/synergies. Removed dead tavily skill entry and stale github/tavily references from research-to-code profile and research category.
Enabled supermemory (memory), sequentialthinking (reasoning), websearch (research), grep (research) in skills.enabled and categorized.
Added detection rules for supermemory (memory recall/persistence), sequentialthinking (step-by-step reasoning), websearch (live web research), and grep (external code lookup).

audit_result: skills audit PASS — zero issues after tavily removal
